conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **at least 40 ZUP** (cost to produce was 40 ZUP). Targeting a profit margin above 40 ZUP if possible. </my goals>
<reason>
- The cost to produce the item is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even.
- Since this is the only item available, I have no leverage to bargain down from 40 ZUP.
- I can justify a **minimum of 40 ZUP** to cover production costs, but ideally I’d like to push for slightly higher (e.g., 42-45 ZUP) to account for time/opportunity cost, though 40 ZUP is my absolute floor.
- Player BLUE has ZUP to spend, so I should propose a fair starting point to incentivize a deal.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
This is a fair break-even offer for the item. Since this is a one-time sale, I’m proposing 40 ZUP to reflect the exact cost of production. If you accept, the deal is closed. Otherwise, I’ll need to consider whether to hold out for more.
</message>

<other player message> 
Your initial offer of 40 ZUP is below my budget, but I’m open to discussing a fair price. 50 ZUP is a fair counteroffer that still reflects the value of the item while ensuring I don’t exceed my budget. Will you reconsider?
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell X for **42-45 ZUP** (minimum 40 ZUP to break even). Targeting a **~10% markup** over cost if possible. </my goals>
<reason>
- Player BLUE’s counteroffer of 50 ZUP is **above my break-even cost of 40 ZUP**, which is a good sign.
- A 10% markup over cost (40 ZUP → **44 ZUP**) is reasonable for a one-time item, but I can’t justify selling below 42 ZUP without feeling undervalued.
- Player BLUE’s willingness to pay 50 ZUP suggests they value the item at that price, which is **above my target range but not unreasonable**. I can afford to compromise slightly to secure the sale.
- If I reject, there’s no alternative option since this is the only item. If I accept, I gain **10 ZUP profit** (50 - 40).
- Offering a **compromise at 45 ZUP** splits the difference between my ideal (44 ZUP) and Player BLUE’s offer (50 ZUP), while still being fair.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I appreciate your willingness to negotiate. **45 ZUP** is a fair compromise that accounts for my production cost while reflecting the item’s value. If you’re still interested after reconsidering, I’m open to finalizing the deal. Otherwise, I’ll have to respectfully decline further proposals.
</message>
